'''Oliver Patrick Gildart''' (born 6 August 1996) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]] and [[Sydney Roosters]] in the Australian [[NRL]], the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the Super League, and spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Workington Town]] in the [[2015 RFL Championship|Kingstone Press Championship]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League. Gildart has also played for {{rlnt|England}} and {{rlnt|Great Britain}} at international level.

Gildart won the 2017 [[Man of Steel Awards|Super League Young Player Of The Year]], and the [[2016 Super League Grand Final|2016]] and the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]]s with Wigan.

==Early life==
Gildart was born in [[Hindley, Greater Manchester]], England to Claire<ref>{{cite web|last=Wilkinson|first=Phil|date=24 May 2017|title=Warriors star's mum and sister, 10, were caught up in terror attack|url=http://www.wigantoday.net/news/warriors-star-s-mum-and-sister-10-were-caught-up-in-terror-attack-1-8559402|publisher=Wigan Today|access-date=13 June 2017}}</ref> and [[Ian Gildart|Ian]], a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer for Wigan, Wakefield, and Oldham.<ref>{{cite web|date=26 December 2012|title=Oliver follows in dad's rugby bootsteps|url=http://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/10121510.Oliver_follows_in_dad_s_rugby_bootsteps|work=Leigh Journal}}</ref> He is of Italian descent through his mother, and is eligible to represent the [[Italy national rugby league team|Italian national team]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Wilkinson|first=Phil|date=30 March 2017|title=Gildart aims to take centre stage|url=http://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/gildart-aims-to-take-centre-stage-1-8465599|publisher=Wigan Today|access-date=13 June 2017}}</ref>

==Club career==
=== 2015-2016 ===
A product of Wigan's junior system, Gildart made his Super League début on loan for [[Salford Red Devils]] in 2015, making a total of three appearances before returning to his parent club.<ref>{{cite web|last=Wilkinson|first=Phil|date=13 May 2015|title=Centre Gildart gained a lot from Salford stint|url=http://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors/centre-gildart-gained-a-lot-from-salford-stint-1-7258664|publisher=Wigan Today|access-date=13 October 2015|archive-date=28 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160828115307/http://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors/centre-gildart-gained-a-lot-from-salford-stint-1-7258664|url-status=dead}}</ref> He made his début for Wigan in the [[Rugby League Super 8s|Super 8s]] match against [[Warrington Wolves]] on 21 August 2015, scoring a try. He then kept his place in the Wigan team for the remainder of the 2015 season, including an appearance in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] at [[Old Trafford]] in only his tenth career match.<ref>{{cite web|last=Walker|first=Gareth|date=8 October 2015|title=Wigan starlet Oliver Gildart grew up around rugby league silverware – now he wants some of his own|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-starlet-oliver-gildart-grew-6597887|work=Mirror}}</ref><ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

Gildart continued to show promise throughout the 2016 season. One of the signature tries of the year was scored by him in an away match in Perpignan as he straightened up the line and seared his way to the try-line after a flowing length-of-the-field attack by Wigan. He became a fixture in the centres for Wigan. Gildart scored a try in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]] which brought Wigan level at 6–6 before they went on to win 12–6 at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

=== 2017 ===
Gildart was in the Wigan team that defeated NRL champions, the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] 22–6 to win the [[World Club Challenge]], which ensured Oliver would join father Ian (who was an interchange in the 1987 and 1991 wins) as a World Club Challenge medal winner. Oliver also scored a try in that game. After starting the season in great form, he broke three vertebrae in his back following a [[Brett Ferres]] 'crusher' tackle. Gildart was ruled out for three months. He returned with a try against the [[Leigh Centurions]] on 8 June. Gildart scored a few weeks later against the [[Warrington Wolves]] followed by a brace (two tries) against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. In the [[Challenge Cup]] semi-final against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], he scored another brace and went on to score another try in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Against traditional rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] he scored the opening try followed by a slick move within the first two minutes. Just a few moments later following a [[Ben Barba]] mistake, Gildart raced forty yards to complete his brace.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=== 2018-2021 ===
[[File:Oli Gildart.jpg|thumb|upright|Gildart playing for the [[Wests Tigers]] in 2022]]
Gildart played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref> In 2019, he played thirty-two games for Wigan, including their shock semi-final loss against Salford.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-salford/52328/|title=Salford seal maiden Super League Grand Final appearance|newspaper=Sky Sports}}</ref> 
Gildart also played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8–4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> On 31 May, Gildart signed a two-year deal to join [[NRL]] side, the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12215/12319561/oliver-gildart-wigan-warriors-centre-to-join-west-tigers-in-nrl-next-season/|title= Wigan Warriors centre to join West Tigers in NRL next season|website=Skysports.com}}</ref> In round 10 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Gildart scored two tries for Wigan in a 18–8 defeat against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57496339|title=Wigan Warriors 8-18 Hull Kingston Rovers|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
In round 1 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Gildart made his club debut for the [[Wests Tigers]] in their 26–16 loss against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-03-12/nrl-roosters-knights-warriors-dragons-tigers-storm/100891316/|title=Melbourne Storm fight back to down Wests Tigers 26-16 after Newcastle, St George Illawarra record NRL wins|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 March 2022 }}</ref> In round 9, he scored his first try in the NRL during a 36–22 loss against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-05-07/nrl-manly-tigers-roosters-titans-cowboys-knights/101042240/|title=North Queensland defeats Newcastle 36-16 for fourth straight NRL win as Sydney Roosters and Manly post victories|newspaper=ABC News|date=7 May 2022 }}</ref> On 31 July, Gildart joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a loan deal which would go to the end of the [[2022 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/oliver-gildart-joins-roosters-short-term-deal-wests-tigers/qudrcn85wp3tt3ug8dksckpn/|title=Oliver Gildart joins Roosters on short-term deal from Wests Tigers|website=Sportingnews.com|date=August 2022 }}</ref> He scored five tries on his debut for [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] in their 60-4 [[NSW Cup]] win over [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-round-23-five-standout-performers-nsw-cup-and-qld-cup/j28djdbgwleqae3efggrpo0t/|title=NRL Round 23: Five standout performers from NSW Cup and QLD Cup|website=Sportingnews.com|date=14 August 2022 }}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
[[File:Oliver Gildart.jpg|thumb|upright|Gildart post-match for England in 2018]]
In October 2022, Gildart was released early from his contract with a year left from the West Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2022-10-27 |title=Wests Tigers release England international Oliver Gildart |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wests-tigers-release-england-international-oliver-gildart/ |access-date=2022-10-27 |website=LoveRugbyLeague |language=en-GB}}</ref> A one-year contract with the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] for 2023 was subsequently confirmed.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/dolphins-confirm-signing-oliver-gildart-after-he-was-released-wests-tigers/js1etyy0sbxqy3dbxrna5aac | title=The Dolphins confirm the signing of Oliver Gildart after he was released by Wests Tigers|website=Sportingnews.com | date=31 October 2022 }}</ref>
Gildart played on the wing for the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in one pre-season NRL trials match. Otherwise, he played as a centre for [[Central Queensland Capras]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. On 1 August, Gildart signed with English side [[Leigh Leopards]] until the end of the [[2013 Super League season results|2023 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/leigh-leopards-confirm-the-signing-of-former-wigan-warriors-centre-oliver-gildart-4239572/|title=Leigh Leopards confirm the signing of former Wigan Warriors centre Oliver Gildart|website=Wigantoday.net}}</ref>

===2025===
On 18 September, Gildart played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 last game of the regular season in victory over Warrington to claim the League Leaders Shield. <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October, Gildart played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan to claim the treble.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 19 February, Gildart played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018, Gildart was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance Squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|website=Loverugbyleague.com|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> Then, the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> His [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut was against Tonga in 2019.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|website=The Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
